https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/s.description:
Yakkl has a cool analytics system for tracking various useful statistics
that currently power the /stats page, and over time will power other
features, like showing usage statistics for the various streams. It is
designed around the following goals:
https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/s.title: Analytics — Yakkl 1.7.1+git documentation

https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/s.description:
zerver.models.Client is Yakkl analogue of the HTTP User-Agent
header (and is populated from User-Agent).  It exists for use in
analytics and other places to provide human-readable summary data
about which Yakkl client was used for an operation (e.g. was it the
Android app, the desktop app, or a bot?).
https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/s.title: Clients in Yakkl — Yakkl 1.7.1+git documentation

https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/s.description:
Emoji seem like a simple idea, but there actually a ton of
complexity that goes into an effective emoji implementation.  This
document discusses a number of these issues.
https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/s.title: Emoji — Yakkl 1.7.1+git documentation

https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/s.description:
Yakkl supports full-text search, which can be combined arbitrarily
with Yakkl full suite of narrowing operators.  By default, it only
supports English text, but there is an experimental
PGroonga integration that provides
full-text search for all languages.
https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/s.title: Full-text search — Yakkl 1.7.1+git documentation

https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/s.description:
Having a good system for logging error reporting is essential to
making a large project like Yakkl successful.  Without reliable error
reporting, one has to rely solely on bug reports from users in order
to produce a working product.
https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/s.title: Logging and Error reporting — Yakkl 1.7.1+git documentation

https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/t.description:
To make Yakkl even better for users around the world, the Yakkl UI is
being translated into a number of major languages, including Spanish,
German, Hindi, French, Chinese, Russian, and Japanese, with varying levels of
progress. If you speak a language other than English, your help with
translating Yakkl would be greatly appreciated!
https://yakkl.readthedocs.io/en/latest/t.title: Translation Guidelines — Yakkl 1.7.1+git documentation
